TITLE.value: Higher Education Student Records Enterprise Reference Model

Scope.value: UK Higher Education — Student Records and associated enterprise systems

Disclaimer.value: This model is a reference framework for higher education institutions. It represents a generalised view of a Student Records ecosystem and should be adapted to reflect the specific systems, integration patterns, and regulatory obligations of the implementing institution. RevelationCore accepts no liability for decisions made on the basis of this model without appropriate institutional validation.

systems.Description: Core SIS functionality to manage student data, enrolment, progression, and awards
systems.Key Capabilities: Enrolment & registration; Academic records; Grades & progression; Graduation & award management

systems.Description: Define and maintain programmes, modules, and learning outcomes
systems.Key Capabilities: Programme/module design; Curriculum change tracking; Learning outcome mapping; Approval workflows

systems.Description: Create and manage academic timetables for classes, rooms, and other resources
systems.Key Capabilities: Schedule generation; Room allocation; Conflict resolution; Calendar integration

systems.Description: Manage interactions and relationships with prospects, students, alumni, partners
systems.Key Capabilities: Lead management; Communication tracking; Recruitment & marketing automation; Stakeholder engagement

systems.Description: Manage library resources, loans, and digital/electronic materials
systems.Key Capabilities: Cataloguing; Circulation/loans; E-resource management; Discovery & search services

systems.Description: Handle financial transactions, budgeting, accounting, and procurement
systems.Key Capabilities: Accounts payable/receivable; Budgeting & forecasting; Procurement; Financial reporting

systems.Description: Provide unified, role-based web access to institutional services and information
systems.Key Capabilities: Single sign-on; Personalised dashboards; Self-service functionality; Communication tools

systems.Description: Track student attendance and engagement indicators; analyse learning activities
systems.Key Capabilities: Attendance tracking; Engagement analytics; Early-warning alerts; Reporting/dashboards

systems.Description: Deliver and manage online learning content, assessments, and collaboration tools
systems.Key Capabilities: Course content hosting; Online assessments; Discussion forums; Gradebook

systems.Description: Manage on-campus accommodation and external event bookings
systems.Key Capabilities: Room allocation; Conference/venue scheduling; Booking/payment processing; Resource allocation

systems.Description: Manage property portfolios, maintenance, space allocation, and campus infrastructure
systems.Key Capabilities: Facilities management; Room allocation; Maintenance scheduling; Capital project management

systems.Description: Provision and manage user identities and access rights across enterprise systems
systems.Key Capabilities: User provisioning; Authentication; Role-based access; Single sign-on

systems.Description: Store, manage, and track electronic documents and records throughout their lifecycle
systems.Key Capabilities: Document version control; Record retention policies; Secure access; Compliance & auditing

systems.Description: Electronic identity verification service for student personal data and documents
systems.Key Capabilities: Document scanning; ID matching; Fraud detection; Verification status updates

systems.Description: Provide data analysis, dashboards, and reporting across institution-wide data sets
systems.Key Capabilities: Data aggregation; Analytics & dashboards; Predictive insights; Performance reporting

systems.Description: Consolidate institutional data from multiple sources for analysis and reporting
systems.Key Capabilities: Data extraction/ETL; Historical data storage; Centralised reporting; Integration with BI tools

systems.Description: Collect and analyse student feedback on modules, lecturers, and teaching effectiveness
systems.Key Capabilities: Feedback surveys; Data analysis & reporting; Action tracking; Quality enhancement

systems.Description: Support human resource processes including recruitment, performance, and payroll setup
systems.Key Capabilities: Staff recruitment & onboarding; Performance reviews; Training & development; Leave management

systems.Description: Execute payroll runs, deductions, tax, and pension management for institutional employees and student bursaries
systems.Key Capabilities: Payroll calculation; Tax & pension contributions; Bursary & stipend processing; Compliance reporting

systems.Description: Centralise and track research outputs, publications, and researcher profiles
systems.Key Capabilities: Publications management; Researcher profiles; Compliance reporting; Collaboration tracking

systems.Description: Support the submission, management, and tracking of research funding and contracts
systems.Key Capabilities: Grant application workflow; Compliance checks; Award management; Contract tracking

systems.Description: Manage and publish digital content for websites, intranets, and portals
systems.Key Capabilities: Web content creation; Content workflow; Version control; Publishing & archiving

systems.Description: Coordinate and track IT support, incidents, requests, and assets
systems.Key Capabilities: Incident management; Service request tracking; Configuration & asset management; Knowledge base

systems.Description: Facilitate system-to-system integration and message brokering between applications
systems.Key Capabilities: Message routing; Data transformation; Enterprise integration patterns; Event-driven architecture

systems.Description: Manage student wellbeing, disability declarations, mental health support, and reasonable adjustments
systems.Key Capabilities: Disability assessment & DSA; Reasonable adjustments management; Mental health case management; Early intervention alerts

systems.Description: Universities and Colleges Admissions Service — the national admissions processing body for UK higher education
systems.Key Capabilities: Application processing; Offer and acceptance management; Clearing coordination; Qualification verification

systems.Description: Higher Education Statistics Agency — statutory body responsible for collecting and publishing data about UK higher education
systems.Key Capabilities: Statutory student data collection; HESA ID assignment; Data quality validation; Sector benchmarking

systems.Description: Government-owned company administering student loans and grants on behalf of the UK government
systems.Key Capabilities: Tuition fee loan processing; Maintenance loan administration; Enrolment confirmation; Overpayment recovery

systems.Description: Home Office agency responsible for student visa compliance; institutions hold a Tier 4 sponsor licence
systems.Key Capabilities: CAS management; Tier 4 attendance monitoring; Visa status tracking; Sponsor compliance reporting

interactions.TEXT: Programme and module catalogue updates
interactions.Description: Curriculum Management is the official source of programmes, modules, and prerequisites. This flow updates the Student Records catalogue with current academic structures, ensuring consistent enrolment, registration, and progression records.

interactions.TEXT: Enrolment, completion, and performance metrics
interactions.Description: SIS delivers enrolment, completion, and performance metrics to Curriculum Management. These analytics inform data-driven updates to programmes, modules, and prerequisites, ensuring academic offerings remain rigorous and aligned with student outcomes.

interactions.TEXT: Student enrolment and module registration data
interactions.Description: Student Records provides key enrolment information needed by the Timetabling system to create accurate class schedules.

interactions.TEXT: Finalised timetable and room allocation data
interactions.Description: The Timetabling system sends finalised scheduling information and related updates back to Student Records, so students and administrative staff have consistent data.

interactions.TEXT: Applicant, offer, and admissions decision data
interactions.Description: The CRM system manages prospective student enquiries, applications, and admissions decisions before they become fully enrolled students. This data flows to Student Records so that the institution can officially register admitted individuals.

interactions.TEXT: Student enrolment status and progression updates
interactions.Description: Once a prospect becomes an enrolled student, Student Records manages their academic progress. Relevant updates flow back to the CRM so the institution can maintain accurate engagement information and manage ongoing communications or alumni relations.

interactions.TEXT: Library loans, fines, and overdue notifications
interactions.Description: The Library system sends circulation updates and overdue notifications to the Student Records system, ensuring that a student's record accurately reflects any outstanding obligations or statuses.

interactions.TEXT: Student enrolment status and access rights
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Library with up-to-date student status and registration data, so the Library system can grant or restrict library privileges accordingly.

interactions.TEXT: Enrolment and tuition fee liability data
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Finance system with key enrolment and fee-related data so the institution can accurately bill students and set up payment plans.

interactions.TEXT: Payment confirmations and financial hold status
interactions.Description: Once payments are made or financial statuses change, Finance sends updates back to Student Records to keep a single source of truth about each student's financial standing.

interactions.TEXT: Student self-service updates and module selections
interactions.Description: The Enterprise Web Portal serves as a central access point for students to submit or update personal information, application details, and module selections.

interactions.TEXT: Enrolment status, timetables, grades, and notifications
interactions.Description: Student Records holds the authoritative information on each student's enrolment, schedule, and academic progress, which is then surfaced in the Enterprise Web Portal for easy access.

interactions.TEXT: Attendance records and absence alerts
interactions.Description: Attendance Monitoring captures real-time or scheduled data about student presence in lectures, seminars, labs, or other learning activities. This data is forwarded to Student Records to keep a consolidated record of each student's engagement.

interactions.TEXT: Student roster and academic calendar data
interactions.Description: Student Records is the main repository of enrolment and course information. This data flows back to Attendance Monitoring so the attendance system knows which students are associated with each class or activity.

interactions.TEXT: Student enrolment, course assignments, and term dates
interactions.Description: Student Records is the authoritative source of enrolment and course information. This data is pushed to the VLE so that the correct students and instructors have access to the right course materials at the right times.

interactions.TEXT: Assessment grades, completion status, and academic alerts
interactions.Description: Once assessments are completed within the VLE, key results flow back into Student Records to maintain an official record of student performance.

interactions.TEXT: Student profiles, enrolment status, and programme details
interactions.Description: Student Records is the authoritative source of official student information. It sends necessary details to the Accommodation system to facilitate housing allocations and ensure eligibility.

interactions.TEXT: Room allocation, booking status, and check-in/out records
interactions.Description: The Accommodation system tracks booking and occupancy details. These updates flow back into Student Records for record-keeping, financial planning, and student support tracking.

interactions.TEXT: Enrolment volumes, timetable data, and occupancy forecasts
interactions.Description: Student Records holds the authoritative enrolment information and academic timetables. The Estates system relies on this data to plan and manage campus facilities effectively.

interactions.TEXT: Room allocation confirmations and availability updates
interactions.Description: Estates captures building usage, room allocations, and event schedules in real time. This information is sent back to Student Records for accurate record-keeping and to inform academic scheduling.

interactions.TEXT: Student identity, enrolment status, and access eligibility
interactions.Description: Student Records is the authoritative source of student data. This system sends key information to IAM so users can be provisioned with appropriate access rights.

interactions.TEXT: Credential updates, account locks, and role assignments
interactions.Description: IAM manages the authentication and authorisation layers for institutional applications. This data flows back to SIS to keep records consistent with login activity and any security flags.

interactions.TEXT: Enrolment forms, transcripts, and graduation certificates
interactions.Description: Student Records is the main source of official student data. Relevant documents are transmitted to the EDRMS for secure storage, version control, and compliance with archival policies.

interactions.TEXT: Archived record confirmations, document links, and access logs
interactions.Description: After documents are archived or updated in the EDRMS, it sends relevant status information or version links back to Student Records so that administrative staff and students can view the most recent files.

interactions.TEXT: Student identity data and verification request
interactions.Description: The Student Records system initiates ID verification checks by sending essential student data and relevant documentation to the Online ID Verification service.

interactions.TEXT: Verification outcome, confidence scores, and fraud flags
interactions.Description: Once the verification process is completed, the Online ID Verification service sends the outcome, along with supporting details, back to the Student Records system.

interactions.TEXT: Student performance, enrolment, and progression data
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Business Intelligence platform with structured extracts of student performance, enrolment volumes, module outcomes, and retention data. This enables institution-wide reporting, benchmarking, and strategic planning.

interactions.TEXT: At-risk flags and predictive intervention alerts
interactions.Description: The BI platform generates predictive analytics and at-risk flags based on cross-system analysis. These alerts are written back to Student Records to enable timely pastoral and academic interventions.

interactions.TEXT: Bulk student data extracts for longitudinal warehousing
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Data Warehouse with full and incremental extracts of student data, including enrolment history, academic outcomes, module registrations, and demographic records, supporting longitudinal analysis and statutory reporting.

interactions.TEXT: Data quality reports and reconciliation alerts
interactions.Description: The Data Warehouse performs cross-system reconciliation and data quality checks. Discrepancy reports and data quality alerts are returned to Student Records to support record accuracy and audit readiness.

interactions.TEXT: Module roster and student registration data for survey distribution
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Student Evaluation of Teaching system with the authoritative list of students registered on each module, enabling targeted and timely survey distribution at the appropriate point in the academic calendar.

interactions.TEXT: Survey completion rates and aggregated feedback scores
interactions.Description: Once surveys close, the Student Evaluation system returns completion statistics and aggregated module-level feedback scores to Student Records, supporting quality assurance processes and academic committee reporting.

interactions.TEXT: Graduate Teaching Assistant records and student-staff roles
interactions.Description: Student Records provides HR with the details of students holding dual roles, such as Graduate Teaching Assistants, research assistants, or student ambassadors. This enables HR to manage employment contracts, right-to-work checks, and payroll setup for student-staff.

interactions.TEXT: Academic supervisor and personal tutor assignments
interactions.Description: HR maintains the authoritative record of staff roles and departmental assignments. This information flows to Student Records to populate supervisor, personal tutor, and academic advisor relationships against each student record.

interactions.TEXT: Bursary-eligible student records and GTA payment authorisations
interactions.Description: Student Records provides Payroll with authorised lists of students eligible for bursaries, hardship funds, or stipend payments, along with GTA hours approved for payment. Enrolment status is included to confirm ongoing eligibility.

interactions.TEXT: Bursary and stipend payment confirmations
interactions.Description: Payroll confirms to Student Records when bursary, stipend, or hardship payments have been processed, allowing the student record to be updated with payment history and supporting financial aid audit trails.

interactions.TEXT: Research student enrolment and researcher profile data
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Current Research Information System with enrolment data for postgraduate research students, enabling CRIS to create and maintain researcher profiles, link students to departments and supervisors, and support REF and open access compliance.

interactions.TEXT: Research degree milestones and publication records
interactions.Description: CRIS returns research progression milestones — such as confirmation of registration, thesis submission, and viva outcomes — along with publication records to Student Records, ensuring that the student's academic record reflects research activity and outputs.

interactions.TEXT: Student researcher eligibility and supervisor assignment data
interactions.Description: Student Records provides Research Proposals with verified details of students named on grant applications, their enrolment status, programme of study, and assigned supervisors, supporting due diligence and funder eligibility checks.

interactions.TEXT: Funded research studentship award records
interactions.Description: Research Proposals confirms to Student Records when a studentship has been awarded, including funder, award value, duration, and any conditions attached. This allows the SIS to associate funding records with the student and supports fee liability management.

interactions.TEXT: Student cohort and programme data for content personalisation
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Content Management System with cohort, programme, and faculty data to enable targeted and personalised content delivery. This allows the CMS to surface programme-specific news, departmental announcements, and relevant regulatory updates to the right student audiences.

interactions.TEXT: Published regulatory and policy notifications
interactions.Description: The CMS notifies Student Records when significant regulatory, policy, or procedural documents are published that require annotation against student records — for example, updated academic regulations, changes to fee policy, or statutory information notices.

interactions.TEXT: Student identity and account data for incident context
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the IT Service Management platform with student identity, enrolment status, and system access details. This enables service desk staff to validate student identity, understand their service entitlements, and contextualise incidents or requests related to student accounts.

interactions.TEXT: Service request outcomes affecting student account status
interactions.Description: ITSM notifies Student Records of resolved service requests or incidents that result in changes to a student's account status, such as account unlocks following security incidents, system access restorations, or completion of data correction requests raised by students.

interactions.TEXT: Application data, offer decisions, and clearing records
interactions.Description: UCAS transmits applicant data, including personal details, qualification records, programme preferences, and offer/acceptance decisions to Student Records. This flow is the primary source of pre-enrolment data for UK undergraduate admissions, including Clearing and Adjustment records.

interactions.TEXT: Enrolment confirmation and withdrawal notifications
interactions.Description: Student Records confirms successful enrolment back to UCAS once an applicant has registered, and notifies UCAS of any withdrawals, deferrals, or no-shows. This data is used by UCAS to update applicant records and release conditional holds on other applications.

interactions.TEXT: Statutory student data return
interactions.Description: Student Records submits the annual statutory HESA Student record return, covering all enrolled students, their programme details, module registrations, qualifications awarded, and demographic data. Accurate and timely submission is a legal requirement and underpins institutional funding allocation and sector benchmarking.

interactions.TEXT: Validation reports and HESA student identifiers
interactions.Description: HESA returns data quality validation reports, error notifications, and the authoritative HESA student identifiers assigned to each student. These identifiers must be written back to Student Records and used in all subsequent statutory submissions and inter-institutional data exchanges.

interactions.TEXT: Enrolment confirmation for tuition fee and maintenance loan release
interactions.Description: Student Records confirms to the Student Loans Company that an eligible student is enrolled and attending, triggering the release of tuition fee loans to the institution and maintenance loan payments to the student. Accurate and timely submission is critical to institutional cash flow and student financial welfare.

interactions.TEXT: Loan entitlement, payment status, and overpayment notifications
interactions.Description: The Student Loans Company notifies Student Records of loan entitlement decisions, payment confirmations, changes to loan status (such as suspension or overpayment recovery), and any queries requiring institutional response. This data supports fee liability management and student financial guidance.

interactions.TEXT: CAS creation requests and Tier 4 attendance data
interactions.Description: Student Records provides UKVI with Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies (CAS) creation requests for international students requiring a Student visa, along with ongoing attendance monitoring data required under the institution's Tier 4 sponsor licence obligations. Failure to report accurately can result in licence suspension.

interactions.TEXT: Visa status updates and sponsor compliance alerts
interactions.Description: UKVI notifies the institution of changes to a student's visa status, including grants, refusals, curtailments, and compliance alerts triggered by attendance or engagement failures. Student Records must be updated promptly to trigger appropriate follow-up action and maintain sponsor licence compliance.

interactions.TEXT: Student profiles, disability declarations, and academic records
interactions.Description: Student Records provides the Student Wellbeing and Disability system with core student data, including declared disabilities, programme details, academic performance indicators, and any existing flags or holds. This enables the wellbeing team to contextualise support needs and ensure continuity of care across the student lifecycle.

interactions.TEXT: Reasonable adjustment plans, DSA records, and support notes
interactions.Description: The Student Wellbeing and Disability system returns approved reasonable adjustment plans, Disabled Students Allowance (DSA) entitlements, mental health support agreements, and case notes to Student Records. These records must be actioned across downstream systems — including the VLE, Attendance Monitoring, and Exams — to ensure legal compliance with the Equality Act 2010.

interactions.TEXT: Learning analytics and digital engagement data
interactions.Description: The VLE provides the Business Intelligence platform with detailed learning analytics, including login frequency, content access patterns, assessment submission behaviours, and discussion forum engagement. This data enriches institutional analytics beyond attendance and grades, supporting early identification of disengaged students.

interactions.TEXT: Attendance trends and engagement analytics
interactions.Description: Attendance Monitoring provides the BI platform with aggregated and individual attendance trend data, absence patterns, and engagement scores. Combined with VLE and academic data, this enables multi-dimensional student retention analytics and supports institutional KPI reporting.

interactions.TEXT: Consolidated multi-system data feeds for analytics
interactions.Description: The Data Warehouse provides the BI platform with consolidated, cleansed, and historicised data drawn from multiple source systems including SIS, Finance, HR, and VLE. This is the primary data feed for strategic dashboards, management information reports, and regulatory benchmarking analysis.

interactions.TEXT: Personalised communications and targeted content
interactions.Description: The CRM system pushes personalised communications, targeted notifications, and campaign content to the Enterprise Web Portal based on student segment, lifecycle stage, or engagement profile. This enables the portal to surface relevant messages — such as open day reminders for applicants or alumni engagement content for graduates — without requiring students to navigate away from their primary portal.

interactions.TEXT: Reasonable adjustments for course content and assessment delivery
interactions.Description: The Student Wellbeing and Disability system pushes approved reasonable adjustments directly to the VLE, including extended submission deadlines, alternative assessment formats, accessible content requirements, and examination accommodations. Timely and accurate propagation of adjustments is a legal obligation under the Equality Act 2010.

interactions.TEXT: Adjusted attendance thresholds and exemption records
interactions.Description: The Student Wellbeing and Disability system notifies Attendance Monitoring of approved adjustments to attendance requirements, including reduced attendance thresholds for students with chronic health conditions, exemptions for mental health absences, and temporary suspensions of attendance alerts during periods of approved medical leave.
